zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren於大約1252年出生在瑞典。她和Gottskalk Van Kyren至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren于大约1252年出生在瑞典。她和Gottskalk Van Kyren至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Kyren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Arie, Avi, Chaja, Eliahu, Eliya, Haim, Hyman, Meyer, Moshe, Ofer.
Dutch and North German: from a short form of Cornelis or Cornelius . This name was also established in Norway from the 16th century onward.

variants.name: Korn
meanings: German: from Middle High German korn ‘grain’, a metonymic occupational name for a factor or dealer in grain or a nickname for a peasant.
Dutch, German, and Czech: from a short form of the personal name Korneli(u)s, Czech Kornel (see Cornelius ).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Korn (Yiddish korn) ‘grain’, a metonymic occupational name as in 1 above, or an artificial name. Compare Corn .

variants.name: Coren
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Gershon, Mayer, Toba.
Americanized form of Jewish, Dutch, Norwegian, Croatian, and Slovenian Koren , Slovak Koreň, and Czech Kořen. This is also an Italianized form of the Croatian and Slovenian name.
Slovenian: variant of   Corn  . This surname is very rare in Slovenia.

historicEvents.title: Earliest Mention of Stockholm
historicEvents.description: The earliest known recorded history of the city of Stockholm is in Norse sagas as Agnafit which dates to 1252. The city rose to prominence due to Baltic trade. Stockholm is now the most populous urban area in the region.

historicEvents.title: The Hatuna Games
historicEvents.description: A conflict on September 29, 1306, between Birger, King of Sweden, and his brothers, the dukes Eric Magnusson and Valdemar Magnusson is known as The Hatuna Games. The brothers arrived to a party late in the night at King Birger's estate. The dukes captured and imprisoned Birger and his wife in Nykoping Castle's dungeon. Duke Eric then gained power over Sweden.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren was born about 1252, in Sweden. She had at least 1 daughter with Gottskalk Van Kyren.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren est née environ 1252, à Suède. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Gottskalk Van Kyren.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren wurde um 1252 in Schweden geboren. Sie hatte mit Gottskalk Van Kyren mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren è nata il circa 1252 nel Svezia. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Gottskalk Van Kyren.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyrenは約1252年にスウェーデンで生まれた。彼女はGottskalk Van Kyrenとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren(은)는 대략 1252년 스웨덴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Gottskalk Van Kyren(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren nasceu aproximadamente 1252, em Suécia. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Gottskalk Van Kyren.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren родилась около 1252 г. в местности Швеция. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Gottskalk Van Kyren.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Gotskalk van Kyren nació aproximadamente 1252, en Suecia. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Gottskalk Van Kyren.
